enforced.oracle: After shuffling all ##meta-information lines (keeping ##fileformat first), the SUT must produce identical canonical output (e.g., parsed JSON representation) as before the transform.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value field order invariance
preconditions: header contains at least one structured ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER line with multiple key=value pairs
enforced.oracle: After reordering key=value pairs inside structured meta-information lines (e.g., Number=1,Type=Integer -> Type=Integer,Number=1), the SUT must produce identical canonical output as before the transform.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.oracle: After trimming common prefix/suffix bases from REF and ALT and adjusting POS accordingly, the variant must be recognized as semantically identical to the original by any normalization-aware parser. The set of called genotypes and allele interpretations must remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

enforced.oracle: Splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele (with synchronized Number=A INFO/FORMAT arrays and remapped GT indices) must produce the same variant set. A spec-compliant parser must treat the split form as equivalent to the multi-ALT form.

evidence.quote: R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

enforced.oracle: Left-shifting an indel in a homopolymer run (decrementing POS by 1, preserving first base of REF/ALT) must produce a variant that is semantically identical to the original. A normalization-aware parser must recognize both representations as the same variant.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a file where ##fileformat is not the first line; silent acceptance or parsing without error is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: GT allele index out-of-bounds rejection invariance
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a record where a GT allele index exceeds the number of ALT alleles (e.g., index 2 on a biallelic record with only one ALT); silent acceptance or record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: missing mandatory fixed column rejection invariance
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a data line that does not contain all 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO); silent acceptance or record-skipping is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: We state explicitly that zero length strings are not allowed, this includes the CHROM and ID column, INFO IDs, FILTER IDs and FORMAT IDs.
All data lines are tab-delimited with no tab character at the end of the line.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality violation rejection invariance
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a cardinality that does not match the number of ALT alleles (e.g., 2 values for a biallelic record with 1 ALT); silent acceptance or truncation is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

enforced.oracle: After round-tripping VCF through BCF2 and back, all POS values, REF allele strings, and END INFO fields (if present) must remain identical. The 1-based coordinate system must be preserved through the binary encoding/decoding cycle. Per VCF v4.5 §6, BCF is a semantically equivalent binary encoding.

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.oracle: After parsing and re-serializing a VCF file through the primary SUT's writer API, all POS values, REF allele strings, and END INFO fields must remain identical. The 1-based coordinate system must be preserved through the write cycle. Per Chen, Kuo, Liu, Tse (2018) §3.2, parse(write(parse(x))) must deep-equal parse(x).

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation preserves query methods for allele counts and structural variant status
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and roundtripping through the SUT writer, public query methods (e.g. getNAlleles(), isStructural(), getAlleleCount()) must return identical values. The number of alleles is preserved (REF + same number of ALTs), and structural variant classification depends on allele content, not ordering.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Information field format] * A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

[VCF v4.5 — Information field format] * R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-information line reordering preserves header query methods
enforced.oracle: After shuffling meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which stays first) and roundtripping through the SUT writer, public header query methods (e.g. getContigNames(), getInfoKeys(), getFormatKeys(), getFilterIds()) must return identical values. The spec explicitly states meta-lines may appear in any order.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed. Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field reordering preserves header query methods
enforced.oracle: After reordering key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines and roundtripping, public query methods (e.g. getInfoFieldType(), getFormatNumber(), getFilterDescription()) must return identical values. The spec mandates implementations must not rely on field ordering.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] To aid human readability, the order of fields should be ID, Number, Type, Description, then any optional fields. Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: BCF round-trip preserves query methods for all record properties
enforced.oracle: After roundtripping VCF through BCF encoding and back, then through the SUT writer, all public query methods (e.g. getNAlleles(), getContig(), getStart(), getEnd(), getInfoValue(), getGenotype()) must return identical values. BCF is the binary equivalent of VCF per spec §6.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value order invariance in data lines
preconditions: at least one data record has multiple semicolon-separated INFO key=value entries
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column of data records, the SUT must produce identical canonical output as before the transform.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote directly addresses structured meta-line fields, not INFO data-line key-value pairs. However, no spec rule mandates ordering of INFO keys in data lines, making reordering semantically neutral.

preconditions: at least 2 sample columns in the VCF header
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the sample column order consistently across the header and all data lines, the SUT must produce identical canonical output (with samples re-identified by their IDs) as before the transform.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; the MR relies on the principle that sample identity is by ID, not column position.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of ##contig, ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER dictionary entries, round-tripping through BCF and back to VCF must produce identical canonical output as before the transform.

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: BCF dictionary order is implementation-defined per VCF v4.5 §6.2.1; a spec-compliant parser must handle any order via the IDX mechanism or implicit indexing.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of GT, Number=A, Number=R, and Number=G fields
quarantine.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping GT indices (0=REF unchanged, 1..N remapped by permutation pi), all Number=A INFO/FORMAT values must be reordered by pi, Number=R values must keep REF first then reorder ALTs by pi, and Number=G values must be reordered per the genotype ordering scheme. The resulting VCF record must be semantically equivalent to the original.

evidence.quote: In general case of ploidy P and N alternate alleles (0 is the REF and 1… N the alternate alleles), the ordering of genotypes for the likelihoods can be expressed by the following pseudocode with as many nested loops as ploidy:
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section <ref> (see Genotype Ordering).
For symbolic structural variant alleles, GT=0 indicates the absence of any of the ALT symbolic structural variants defined in the record.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing-value injection round-trip preserves VCF semantics
quarantine.oracle: Appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing values) to all samples must produce a semantically equivalent VCF. Missing values are semantically equivalent to the field being absent.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Genotype fields] If genotype information is present, then the same types of data must be present for all samples. First a FORMAT field is given specifying the data types and order (colon-separated FORMAT keys matching the regular expression , duplicate keys are not allowed). This is followed by one data block per sample, with the colon-separated data corresponding to the types specified in the format.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that adding a FORMAT field with all missing values is semantically equivalent to omitting it, but missing values ('.') are defined as representing unknown/absent data.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffling preserves record query methods
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value entries in the INFO column and roundtripping, public record query methods (e.g. getInfoValue(), hasInfoKey(), getInfoKeys()) must return identical values. The INFO field is a set of key-value pairs with no ordering semantics.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed. Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO field key-value pairs on data lines have no ordering semantics, but by analogy with meta-line ordering and structured field ordering, a compliant parser should treat them as an unordered set.

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves per-sample query methods
quarantine.oracle: After permuting sample columns and roundtripping, public query methods that access samples by ID (e.g. getSample(), getGenotype(), getSampleData()) must return identical values when addressed by sample name. The sample identity is tied to the column header ID, not its position.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] Genotype fields. If genotype information is present, then the same types of data must be present for all samples. First a FORMAT field is given specifying the data types and order... This is followed by one data block per sample, with the colon-separated data corresponding to the types specified in the format.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample columns may be reordered, but sample identity is by column header name, not position. A parser that indexes samples by column position rather than name would be non-compliant.
